data.executiveSummary: The qa-login service experienced significant failures due to redis connection timeouts, impacting user login sessions.

rootCause.title: Redis Connection Timeout
rootCause.description: The qa-login service encountered repeated exceptions related to Redis connection timeouts. This prevented successful retrieval of session data, resulting in login failures.

evidenceChain: Error logs show repeated instances of 'Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.'
Prometheus metrics indicate a high error rate of 27.89% for HTTP 500 statuses in the qa-login service.

timeline.event: LoginException in qa-login: Session store retrieval failure. Redis connection timeout after 5000ms.

data.impact: Users were unable to log in as expected due to session retrieval failures, resulting in a high error rate and potential service downtime.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Restart the Redis instance to clear any orphaned connections, as resolved in similar incident KAN-6.
Implement monitoring and alerting on Redis connection pool usage to prevent future timeouts.
Investigate Redis configuration and client settings to ensure efficient connection management.
